title: NorthStar Systems
preview: Lost to AWS SageMaker managed endpoints due to lower procurement friction, existing cloud commit discounts, security team's preference for native logging/KMS, and lack of internal champion after departure of ML platform lead.
text: Lost to AWS SageMaker managed endpoints due to lower procurement friction, existing cloud commit discounts, security team's preference for native logging/KMS, and lack of internal champion after departure of ML platform lead.

title: Cedarline Systems
preview:
CRM notes/discovery:
- Account: Cedarline Systems (medical devices firmware + clinical analytics). Highly regulated (FDA) — cannot send PHI outside corporate VPC.
- Primary ask: enterprise IDE copilot with guaranteed sub-100ms token stream for in-IDE completions, ability to pin models per team, and strict audit trails.
- Prefers private control plane sitting in their AWS account (VPC endpoint), occasional on-prem for air-gapped test rigs.
- SE feedback: workload is high-throughput small-completions (code completions, signature generation, unit test scaffolding) — batching strategy must preserve low tail latency.
- Security: SSO via SAML (Okta), audit logs to their Splunk, keys in their KMS, 7-year retention on code review artifacts per compliance.
- Quote from CTO: "We need deterministic inference behavior across dev and CI — model pinning + rollout control is non-negotiable."
- POC plan: 4-week eval — week1 smoke in dev VPC, week2 IDE plugin latency test, week3 canary across 2 teams, week4 security validation and SLA test.
- Current blockers: Legal drafting custom data residency clause; procurement needs SOC2 Type II report + penetration test summary.
- Demo notes: showed streaming in VS Code plugin; they flagged token-level cost breakdown as useful for internal chargeback.
- Next meetings: contract sketch to legal (Jordan), on-prem install window proposal (SE Priya) — aim to sign in Feb 2025.
- Artifacts: security questionnaire submitted (drive link above), sent initial SOW draft.
- Internal notes: consider offering dedicated reserved capacity with burst to hosted for disaster recovery; map SKU to their throughput profile.

title: Backstop Commerce
preview:
Company overview: Backstop Commerce is a mid-market e-commerce platform focused on post-purchase customer experience (returns, exchanges, shipping updates) across multiple storefronts. Primary users for Redwood are Product/Engineering (chat agents) and Ops (peak capacity planning).

Sales/Eng summary: evaluating Hosted API first to validate latency SLOs for streaming chat + tool-calling, then considering Dedicated for predictable peak season capacity and data residency. Cost-sensitive but willing to commit if Dedicated demonstrates unit cost improvements under high-concurrency workloads.

Key requirements / success metrics:
- p95 latency target: <= 250ms for streaming partials during normal traffic, <= 350ms absolute cap during peak spikes; overall want consistent p95 across customer sessions.
- Peak season scaling: Black Friday/Cyber Monday projected 4-6x baseline concurrency; need autoscale/burst plan and predictable tail-latency.
- Tool calling: agent must call order API, returns system, and shipment tracking webhooks reliably within flow (sync tool calls <500ms preferred).
- Session affinity / KV cache: reuse KV cache across agent sessions to reduce token costs and latency.
- Observability: token-level latency and per-route p95 dashboards, retention for 6 months for audits.

Recent timeline (CRM-style):
- 2025-10-20: inbound interest (product) via website; AE assigned (Jordan).
- 2025-10-27: discovery call (Jordan + Maya) — high-level requirements, demo request.
- 2025-11-03: hosted API demo showing streaming + tool-calls; internal tech lead joined. Demo notes: streaming partials looked snappy; Redwood demo p95 ~180ms for short-turn utterances in controlled env.
- 2025-11-05: technical deep-dive FF call (ff-20251105-7842) — discussed KV caching, fallback model variants, routing by latency tier.
- 2025-11-12: security questionnaire kickoff (ff-20251112-1120) — legal wants SOC2 + ISO details, KMS options, retention controls. Jira SEC-77 opened for encryption questions.
- 2025-11-15: requested hosted POC plan and pricing ranges (see drive POC deck).

Customer quotes / notes from stakeholders:
- "We can't let p95 spikes exceed 350ms during peak — that drives retries and CS load" — Head of Engineering.
- "If hosted proves reliable under our simulated holiday traffic, Dedicated pricing/commit would be straightforward" — Head of Product.

POC scope (agreed):
- 2-week hosted POC: instrument streaming chat + tool-calling against a synthetic load that simulates 5x holiday peak; capture p50/p95/p99 for token streaming, tool-call latencies, and end-to-end completion time.
- Evaluate fallback behavior: model variant routing (cheaper/quantized) when capacity constrained.
- Validate KV caching impact on average token count and latency.

Open questions / blockers:
- Security: legal/infra need SOC2 + ISO docs and KMS architecture diagram (SEC-77).
- Traffic generation: need Redwood-supplied load script or plan to reproduce Black Friday pattern.
- Pricing: prefer committed Dedicated pricing comparison (monthly commit) vs hosted per-token at projected traffic; need model for break-even.

Next steps (short):
- Redwood: deliver hosted POC plan & load script, final POC dates, and Dedicated pricing ranges (Jordan).
- Backstop: schedule security review slot, provide sample order API endpoints for tool-calling POC, and grant test data access behind VPC if needed.

Linked artifacts: drive://Backstop/Redwood/POC-Plan-v1 (POC steps + load profile), drive://Backstop/Redwood/Security-FAQ (SOC2/ISO answers).

Notes formatting: CRM shorthand, expect additional legal questions after POC.

title: Banyan Spark Finance
preview:
Lead source: inbound via blog post + free credits. CTO (Rhea Kapoor) demoed prototype last week.

Summary: small payments startup building merchant dashboard + reconciliations. Wants to add contextual AI for: quick transaction summaries for ops, automated support replies, and embeddings for internal docs. Mainly interested in hosted API to get started — 'fastest path to production' per Rhea. VPC-curious: asked about a lightweight VPC peering option later.

Security asks (early): SOC2 report, SSO/SAML for admin console, audit logs, KMS integration. Mentioned regulator in onboarding (money transmitter in 2 states) — not full enterprise yet but sensitive.

Performance targets: 50 rps peaks for support chat (p95 latency < 350ms desired), embedding queries ~200/day, reranking for fraud signals latency OK at 200-300ms. Cost sensitivity: high; wants predictable per-token guidance and batching suggestions.

Timeline & recent activity:
- 2025-01-28: inbound signup, free credits applied.
- 2025-01-29: intro call (FF-20250129-4471) — demo of current flow, captured example prompts.
- 2025-02-12: AE Maya sent usage recommendations and quickstart.
- 2025-02-25: CTO follow-up — asked about SOC2 + SSO; requested sample DPA.
- 2025-03-02: Discovery call with Maya + SE Jordan (FF-20250302-8492). SE captured sample traffic profile and latency targets.

Quotes / paraphrases:
- Rhea: 'We need something we can ship in weeks, but we can't ignore compliance.'
- PM: 'If we can get SSO and an attestation, we'll move past legal.'

POC ask: 2-week trial using hosted_api with sample dataset, measure p95 latency and token cost. Will evaluate caching suggestions and batching. SE to propose minimal config and recommended model family with cost estimate.

Next steps (short):
- Send SOC2 attestation, SSO integration guide, sample DPA (Maya).
- SE Jordan to draft POC plan + token cost estimate by 2025-03-07.
- Schedule POC kickoff (30m) week of Mar 10.

Notes style: fragments, action-oriented. Follow-ups tracked in Gmail thread and Drive pricing deck.

title: Ravenwood Cortex Solutions
preview:
Summary: deal stalled and effectively closed-lost due to sponsor departure and company re-org. Original champion (VP of ML, Marcus Hale) left 2025-02-05; hiring freeze and product prioritization shift to infrastructure costs.

Timeline / recent activity:
- 2024-09-10: First intro call (Jordan B.) — high interest in embeddings for search + summarization POC.
- 2024-10-22: Demo with SE (Priya) — liked streaming + routing controls. Requested benchmark vs Anthropic.
- 2024-11-18: Security review kickoff (linked Fireflies: ff_2024-11-18_8245). Provided SOC2 questionnaire and partial KMS docs.
- 2024-12-02: Short POC started (search rerank + caching). POC metrics: latency ~120ms p95 on hosted test, cost above target by ~40%.
- 2025-01-15: Procurement asked for private deployment + audit — asked for quote for on-prem option and SLA. (drive:/deals/ravenwood/prod-fit-deck.pdf)
- 2025-02-05: Sponsor (Marcus Hale) left company — internal note: "They reorganized ML under infra, new head deprioritizing customer-facing AI".
- 2025-02-11: AE attempted re-engagement; received terse reply: "Pausing all external vendor evaluations until Q3. No one owns this now." (thread-CA123xy789)
- 2025-02-12: Deal moved to inactive/closed-lost bucket in CRM.

Key requirements & fit:
- Use cases: customer support summarization, semantic doc search, reranking for knowledge base.
- Latency target: p95 < 150ms for chat/search; throughput 500 QPS peak for global support.
- Cost sensitivity: target ARR economics required < $200k/year; POC showed higher unit cost without aggressive batching or quantization.
- Security: strict residency for EU customers, SOC2 required, SSO/SAML + audit logs + KMS integration. Private/VPC preferred for procurement.

Why stalled / lost:
- No internal champion after VP ML departure. No clear successor assigned to own vendor evaluations.
- Company-wide hiring and budget freeze hitting vendor spend; procurement paused all evals.
- Security asked for on-prem audit that would take extra 8-10 weeks and additional cost — blocked timelines.
- Competitors (OpenAI/Anthropic/Cohere) offered trial credits and faster commercial terms which lowered near-term risk for procurement.

Sales notes / playbook items:
- Attempt re-engagement in Q3 if hiring freeze lifts; target new Head of ML/CTO.
- Keep SOC2 + KMS package updated in drive link; prepare a short one-page re-org briefing for execs.
- Capture POC logs and cost-tuning suggestions (quantization and batching) for a future follow-up to show unit cost parity.
- Marked for nurture cadence: quarterly check-ins and monthly security digest until re-opened.

Quotes / direct lines from buyer:
- "We're pausing all vendor evaluations until leadership settles — sorry, not something I can push right now." — Procurement lead
- "If we get a sponsor back, reach out — we liked the demo, but timelines shifted." — Senior Eng.

Next steps (closed):
- No active champion, deal closed-lost on 2025-02-12 due to org churn. AE to log lessons and archive POC artifacts. Reopen criteria: clear sponsor assigned + budget reallocated.

title: Oakridge Intelligence Group
preview:
Status: Closed - lost to hyperscaler managed endpoints (AWS).

Summary bullets:
- Late-stage procurement; heavy emphasis from procurement and infra on reducing vendor footprint.
- POC (embeddings + semantic search) ran on Redwood Dedicated in a single VPC subnet (POC cluster spun for 2 weeks). Latency and relevance metrics met internal SLOs.
- Cost model: Redwood estimated ~30-40% TCO advantage at forecasted scale (with caching + batching) but procurement prioritized single-cloud consolidation and integrated billing.
- Security review: Redwood passed technical answers; security team flagged support burden of a second inference vendor despite VPC option. Quote from SecOps: "we can't add another inference vendor into our stack right now — enterprise AWS contract covers managed endpoints and support." 
- Decision: Oakridge procurement selected AWS-managed endpoints (SageMaker/Bedrock) to keep vendor count down and to leverage existing enterprise discount and monitoring integrations.

Timeline (recent activity):
- 2024-11-12: Intro discovery call (Jordan B.) — high-level requirements, use-case fit (embeddings + underwriting chat).
- 2024-11-29: Technical deep dive with SE (Maya C.) — runtime/latency expectations, VPC connectivity discussed.
- 2024-12-08: POC kickoff — Redwood provided dedicated test pool and sample connectors into S3/RedShift.
- 2025-01-18: Security questionnaire submitted; follow-up Q&A with SecOps.
- 2025-01-22: POC wrap & demo (fireflies ff-2025-01-22-oi-poceval). Results: relevance + latency OK; cost projection disputed by procurement.
- 2025-02-06: Procurement decision meeting — confirmed preference for AWS-managed endpoints.
- 2025-02-10: Final email from procurement. "We will onboard with AWS endpoints to simplify ops and unify support. Open to revisit at renewal."

Why we lost (main drivers):
- Procurement policy requiring cloud-native / managed endpoints where possible to reduce vendor management overhead.
- Existing enterprise AWS contract + internal incentives (billing/observability/managed support) made hyperscaler option administratively simpler.
- No strong internal champion to fight consolidation decision — infra prioritized least operational friction.

Objections raised from Oakridge that were not fully resolved:
- Concern about multi-cloud vendor sprawl (their priority) vs. Redwood argument for cost/latency tradeoffs.
- Questions around single-signoff for incident response and vendor SLAs; they preferred a single hyperscaler support path.

Next actions for Redwood (internal):
- Mark account closed-lost; record hyperscaler as primary loss reason.
- Set reminder for 6-month renewal window and account health check (possible re-engage if AWS experience is poor or for non-core workloads).
- Add POA: create a short note bundle comparing Redwood Dedicated + VPC vs. AWS managed endpoints with clear ops and cost tradeoffs for future AE outreach.

Stubs/links: pricing deck in Drive, security questionnaire spreadsheet, fireflies transcript id included above.

title: Onyx PulseEdge Technologies
preview: Realtime ad-serving platform needs a dedicated inference tier for reranking + embedding scoring. Targets: p95 latency <45-50ms at 40 QPS sustained, graceful fallback to smaller quantized models, prefix/KV caching for common user histories, intelligent batching to reduce cost without breaking SLOs. Must pass SOC2/legal, support VPC + customer KMS, and provide a perf benchmarking harness + reproducible cost model. Interested in Redwood Optimize recommendations (batching/caching/quantization) and a Dedicated offering with predictable billing.

Must pass SOC2/legal, support VPC + customer KMS, and provide a perf benchmarking harness + reproducible cost model. Interested in Redwood Optimize recommendations (batching/caching/quantization) and a Dedicated offering with predictable billing.

title: DiffSmith Labs
preview: Quick notes from calls and async: - Product: lightweight CI-integrated PR reviewer that runs on prereceive or as a GitHub action; focuses on diff summarization, suggested comments, and reranking of suggested fixes. - Customer: engineering-heavy startup, distributed teams, heavy use of monorepo. Priorities: sub-200ms token streaming in IDE, reliable per-PR reranking, model pinning for deterministic reviews. - Quote (CTO): 'We need repeatable, debuggable suggestions — not hallucinations.' - Security: must support SSO (SAML), KMS for key management, and ability to route sensitive repos to Dedicated/VPC. - Cost sensitivity: willing to pay for predictable dedicated capacity if latency/residency requirements met. - Current stack: GitHub Enterprise, internal CI, SSO via Okta. - Legal: early redlines around retention and right-to-export; legal will push SLA language.

Priorities: sub-200ms token streaming in IDE, reliable per-PR reranking, model pinning for deterministic reviews. - Quote (CTO): 'We need repeatable, debuggable suggestions — not hallucinations.' - Security: must support SSO (SAML), KMS for key management, and ability to route sensitive repos to Dedicated/VPC. - Cost sensitivity: willing to pay for predictable dedicated capacity if latency/residency requirements met. - Current stack: GitHub Enterprise, internal CI, SSO via Okta. - Legal: early redlines around retention and right-to-export; legal will push SLA language.

title: Skyloom Systems
preview: Mid-market B2B SaaS evaluating Hosted -> Dedicated. In active procurement; negotiating Dedicated commit levels, burst cap, overage mechanics, and ramp schedule. POC showed good latency; security and legal redlines are main blockers.

title: BerryBind Marketworks

title: Foxmere Product Intelligence
preview:
Context: mid-market product analytics vendor. Wants to replace an expensive vector DB + hosted llm combo. Primary drivers: reduce inference unit cost, keep sub-200ms assistant latency for in-app use, and meet SOC2/KMS requirements. POC scope: embeddings for product docs search + session-based in-app assistant. Evaluation path: start Hosted API for rapid integration -> if throughput/cost favorable move to Dedicated reserved capacity.

POC / Tech notes:
- POC dataset: ~120k doc chunks (product docs, release notes) + session logs for assistant routing.
- Targets: 150 qps embedding requests (batched), 0.2s per-token latency target for assistant happy-path, 95th pct latency < 500ms.
- Cost sensitivity: wants to halve current per-embedding cost and reduce assistant token cost by ~40%.

Workshops & findings (11/21 workshop):
- Batching: Recommend adaptive batching windows; estimated 1.8x throughput improvement on embeddings at 200ms batch window.
- Caching: KV-prefix caching for repeated session prompts can save ~25% token compute in flows with UI-level repetition. POC to enable prefix cache for top-20 queries.
- Quantization: Tested 4-bit quantized variant on dev - 10-18% latency improvement, slight throughput variance; quality drop within tolerance for retrieval+assistant route but needs final eval on release notes dataset.
- Unit economics: Built a sample model showing hosted costs @ current config ~ $9k/month; with batching+cache+quantization expected down to $4.8k/month; Dedicated reserved pool projected to drop unit cost further but requires 12-month commit.

Quotes / customer color: "We like Redwood's rollout guardrails and the ability to pin model variants for canarying. Need clear numbers for Dedicated vs hosted break-even."

Open items / next actions:
- Finish security questionnaire (security team to return SSO/KMS answers) - due 2024-12-03.
- Finalize POC SOW and measurement metrics (Maya + Foxmere engineering) - target 2024-12-05.
- Pricing decision: run 2-week hosted-run POC with Optimize recommendations enabled; then re-evaluate Dedicated pricing (AE to provide committed-capacity options).

title: Lacuna Mail Automate
preview:
Snapshot from deal playbook and call notes:
- Company background: mid-sized ecommerce marketplace (apparel, ~350 employees). Heavy email + Zendesk ticket volume (~12k/mo). Peak spikes during promos (Black Friday spikes ~6x baseline).
- Primary ask: deploy summarization + auto-tagging that plugs into Zendesk macros/workflows. Want concise 2-3 line summaries for CS UI and reliable tags to drive routing and macros.
- Performance targets: per-ticket summarization ideally <500ms in UI; background batch auto-tagging throughput target 300-500 tickets/sec during reprocess windows. Latency for agent-assist stream can be 150-300ms for real-time suggestions.
- Model preferences: open to thin-hosted models (quantized) or Redwood Dedicated. Preference to avoid full public-prod LLM calls for PII. Interested in model fallback policy (smaller faster model during spikes).
- Reliability: must withstand 6x traffic spikes; want autoscale + graceful fallback (route to lightweight model or queued batch).
- Security/compliance: require SSO/SAML, audit logs surfaced in console, KMS integration, 90-day retention for sensitive fields, EU data residency for EU customers.
- Integration ask: Zendesk app (private listing) + Freshdesk compatibility considered. Need a manifest sample for internal security review. Must support Zendesk triggers/macros and Freshdesk automations out of the box or via simple webhooks.
- Data for POC: exported 10k tickets (anonymized) uploaded to drive link. SE asked for schema + sample prompt set.
- Quote from head of CS: "We need summaries that save the agent time — not longer, fluffier text."
- Stakeholders: Head of CS (decision), VP Eng (budget approval), InfoSec (security sign-off).
- Decision timeline: pilot this month, procurement by Dec if pilot metrics met.
- Short-term POC success metrics: 30% avg agent time savings on triage, 85% accuracy on top-5 auto-tags, sustained 95th pct latency under 800ms during synthetic spike test.
- Practical notes / choppy CRM shorthand: "SE to provide prompt set + sample macro templates. AE to share Dedicated vs Hosted TCO by 11/29. InfoSec sent SOC2 + KMS checklist, awaiting answers. Need Zendesk app manifest before infra sign-off."

title: Amberleaf AI Atelier
preview:
Inbound SMB lead. Tech profile: Next.js frontend hosted on Vercel, backend microservices in Node + occasional Python workers. Primary workload: interactive code-assistant embedded in docs + search/rerank for snippets. Wants low-friction hosted API (self-serve) — already on trial. 

Pain points observed: streaming behavior in Node examples inconsistent; LangChain \"chain\" wiring confusing for their team (mostly JS engineers); need LlamaIndex-style indexing example for product docs + a small-scale vector store (they use Supabase/Postgres but open to Pinecone). Cost sensitivity: chat-heavy, expects many short sessions per user -> asks about batching/kv-cache tips and token-minimizing prompt templates. 

Security/compliance: SMB but client-facing enterprise customers so wants audit logs and key rotation policies; will need a short appendix for their security team (SOC2 status, how to forward audit logs, KMS integration sketch). 

Quoted from call: \"We want a one-repo quickstart we can clone and drop into our dev pipeline — Node Next.js + a python worker for offline embeddings. If you give us a clear LangChain/LlamaIndex adapter and a Vercel streaming example, we can ship an MVP in 2 weeks.\"

Requested artifacts: LangChain adapter (JS), LlamaIndex example (py) using Redis/Supabase vector backend stub, Vercel AI SDK streaming snippet, sample cost analysis for 50k monthly requests. 

Internal notes: prioritize lightweight examples (<= 300 LOC), include token-cost best practices (prefix caching, short system prompts, partial codex-style prompts). Customer likely to convert if we deliver working repo + 30m pairing session quickly.
